meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Fire at key Australian refinery deepens fuel risks amid Iran war shortages
items.summary: A major fire at a key refinery in Australia refinery has disrupted fuel production, raising fears of fuel shortages.

items.title: Muslims the target? Fury as millions lose voting rights in India’s Bengal
items.summary: Key eastern state heads to polls under the shadow of controversial voter list revision, which many say excludes Muslims.

items.title: Why many Kashmiris are donating gold, breaking piggy banks for Iran
items.summary: A centuries-old historical bond has come alive again amid the war on Iran, with Kashmiris sharing whatever they can.

items.title: US military kills three in new Eastern Pacific boat strike
items.summary: The attack is the latest in a string of killings by the United States that rights groups say are 'unlawful'.

items.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 48 of the US-Iran conflict?
items.summary: US–Iran talks gain pace as Pakistan mediates, optimism for a new round of talks in Islamabad.

items.title: Curry scores 35 as Warriors upset Clippers to extend playoff run
items.summary: Stephen Curry's Golden State Warriors keep their NBA playoff hopes alive, eliminating LA Clippers from the play-in.

items.title: Israel’s military kills four Lebanese paramedics in consecutive strikes
items.summary: An Israeli triple-tap attack on rescue workers in southern Lebanon has killed four and left several others injured.

items.title: The pope has shown the world how to stand up to Trump
items.summary: Far too many world leaders have chosen to mollify the bullying US president. Pope Leo has shown there's another way.

items.title: Trump plans 250-foot ‘Triumphal Arch’ to mark 250 years of US independence
items.summary: The Trump administration has unveiled plans for a huge structure to mark 250 years since the US declared independence.

items.title: Trump says Israel and Lebanon’s leaders will speak on Thursday
items.summary: No official response from Israel or Lebanon to US statement, which comes after Netanyahu ordered expansion of invasion.

items.title: Hopes grow for a breakthrough in US-Iran talks as Pakistan mediates
items.summary: Pakistani sources say there has been a 'major breakthrough' over a deal on Iran’s nuclear programme.

items.title: Democrats clash with US Energy Secretary over Iran war and gas prices
items.summary: Watch the moment a Democratic congresswoman tells the US Energy Secretary he is ‘living in a different world’.

items.title: Pakistan expecting ‘major breakthrough’ tied to Iran’s nuclear programme
items.summary: A source has told Al Jazeera that Pakistan is expecting a breakthrough tied to Iran’s nuclear programme.

items.title: Israeli forces fire stun grenades at journalists in occupied Nablus
items.summary: Video shows Israeli forces firing stun grenades towards journalists who were reporting on the army’s raid of Nablus.

items.title: Brazil’s fugitive ex-spy chief freed in the US after ICE detention
items.summary: Alexandre Ramagem, who fled Brazil following his conviction for plotting a coup, was held by ICE in Florida on Monday.

items.title: Russian attacks on Ukraine kill 14, including a child, authorities say
items.summary: Deadly attacks hit Kyiv, Odesa, and Dnipro, while Russia says two children killed in Ukrainian attacks.

items.title: Huge crowds greet Pope Leo in Cameroon 20 years after outreach trip
items.summary: Huge crowds have greeted Pope Leo in Cameroon, returning to a country he visited 20 years ago as ‘Father Bob’.

items.title: Iran war live: Pakistan in push for new round of US-Iran peace negotiations
items.summary: At least 2,167 people have been killed and more than 7,000 injured in Israeli attacks on Lebanon since March 2.

items.title: El Salvador publishes law allowing life sentences for minors as young as 12
items.summary: The new sentencing measure comes as President Nayib Bukele oversees a four-year-long state of emergency against crime.

items.title: Peru’s ballot count drags on amid frustration with presidential election
items.summary: The turbulent election has prompted fraud claims by candidates and frustration with the country's chaotic politics.

items.title: Israel bombs homes in southern Lebanon
items.summary: The Israeli army has bombed buildings in southern Lebanon’s Bint Jbeil district.

items.title: Brazil’s police open a probe into presidential candidate Flavio Bolsonaro
items.summary: Investigation centres on whether Bolsonaro committed defamation by linking Lula to crimes like drug trafficking.

items.title: Bayern knock Real Madrid out of Champions League in quarterfinal classic
items.summary: Luis Diaz and Michael Olise score late goals in Munich to give Bayern a dramatic 6-4 aggregate win over Real Madrid.

items.title: US jury finds Ticketmaster and Live Nation had anticompetitive monopoly
items.summary: The verdict could cost Live Nation and Ticketmaster hundreds of millions of dollars.

items.title: Could the EU’s alliance with Israel soon change?
items.summary: A European Union petition is on track to trigger a formal review of the EU-Israel Association Agreement.
